relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Moses gathers the whole congregation for the commanded work.
Before craft begins, Sabbath rest is made holy.
Even fire-making pauses on Sabbath.
The contribution command is spoken.
Only the willing heart brings YHWH’s contribution.
Sacred colors and linen are offered.
Skins and acacia wood are offered.
Oil and spices are offered for light and fragrance.
Stones are offered for priestly remembrance.
Wise-hearted makers are called forward.
The dwelling and its structure are named.
Ark, cover, and veil are named.
Table and bread of presence are named.
Lampstand, lamps, and oil are named.
Incense altar, oil, incense, and entrance screen are named.
Burnt altar, basin, and vessels are named.
Court hangings and gate screen are named.
Pegs and cords are remembered too.
Holy garments for priestly service are named.
The people go out from Moses’ presence.
Lifted hearts and willing spirits bring the work’s materials.
Men and women bring gold with willing hearts.
Those who have sacred fabrics and hides bring them.
Silver, bronze, and acacia wood are contributed.
Wise-hearted women spin with their hands.
Women lifted in wisdom spin goats’ hair.
Leaders bring stones for ephod and breastpiece.
Spices and oil come for light, anointing, and incense.
Every willing man and woman brings freewill offering.
Bezalel is called by name.
Spirit of Divine fills him with wisdom, understanding, knowledge, and craft.
He designs work in gold, silver, and bronze.
Stone and wood receive skilled craft.
Bezalel and Oholiab receive hearts to teach.
Wisdom of heart fills every craft and design.

contemplative_note: Exodus 35 is sacred making from free hearts: Sabbath first, then offerings, women’s wisdom, artisan teaching, and Spirit-filled craft.
